title: Astracantha oechtoeranensis
intro: Astracantha oechtoeranensis, also known as Ochotona Astragalus or Astragalus oechtoeranensis, is a type of perennial legume plant belonging to the Astragalus genus. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine to treat various health conditions, including respiratory problems, cardiovascular issues, and inflammation, due to its anti-inflammatory, antioxidant, and immunomodulatory properties. The bioactive constituents of Astracantha oechtoeranensis include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its therapeutic effects. In traditional medicine, Astracantha oechtoeranensis is often prepared as a decoction, infusion, or tincture, which can be used to treat coughs, colds, and other respiratory ailments.
taxonomy: Astracantha oechtoeranensis belongs to the family Fabaceae. It is a species of legume. The genus Astracantha is a subgenus of the genus Caragana. Astracantha oechtoeranensis is native to Turkey. It is a type of shrub that is often found in dry environments.
